title: Video Stream Connection Errors and Quality Issues (Meraki MV)
dataSources: `index=meraki sourcetype=meraki:assurancealerts` from `cisco_meraki_assurance_alerts` plus `index=meraki sourcetype=meraki:webhook` from `cisco_meraki_webhook` in `Splunk_TA_cisco_meraki` (Splunkbase 5580). Stream-quality issues surface as assurance alerts; specific connection errors come via webhook.
spl:
index=meraki (sourcetype="meraki:assurancealerts" OR sourcetype="meraki:webhook")
| where like(deviceTypes, "%camera%") OR like(deviceType, "%camera%") OR productType="camera"
| stats count as alert_count, latest(_time) as last_alert by deviceName, alertType, severity
| where alert_count > 1
| sort - alert_count
description: Detects video stream connection failures that prevent remote viewing or recording.
value: Detects video stream connection failures that prevent remote viewing or recording.
implementation: Use `cisco_meraki_assurance_alerts` (default 1h poll) for camera-related assurance alerts (stream connection issues, RTSP failures, NVR communication errors). For real-time per-event alerts also configure `cisco_meraki_webhook` and subscribe to camera alert types in the Dashboard.
visualization: Connection error timeline; affected camera list; error type breakdown.
knownFalsePositives: Sensor anomalies during cleaning, calibration, or planned environmental changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=meraki sourcetype=meraki:assurancealerts` from `cisco_meraki_assurance_alerts` plus `index=meraki sourcetype=meraki:webhook` from `cisco_meraki_webhook` in `Splunk_TA_cisco_meraki` (Splunkbase 5580). Stream-quality issues surface as assurance alerts; specific connection errors come via webhook..
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Use `cisco_meraki_assurance_alerts` (default 1h poll) for camera-related assurance alerts (stream connection issues, RTSP failures, NVR communication errors). For real-time per-event alerts also configure `cisco_meraki_webhook` and subscribe to camera alert types in the Dashboard.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=meraki (sourcetype="meraki:assurancealerts" OR sourcetype="meraki:webhook")
| where like(deviceTypes, "%camera%") OR like(deviceType, "%camera%") OR productType="camera"
| stats count as alert_count, latest(_time) as last_alert by deviceName, alertType, severity
| where alert_count > 1
| sort - alert_count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Video Stream Connection Errors and Quality Issues (Meraki MV)** ‚Äî Detects video stream connection failures that prevent remote viewing or recording.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=meraki sourcetype=meraki:assurancealerts` from `cisco_meraki_assurance_alerts` plus `index=meraki sourcetype=meraki:webhook` from `cisco_meraki_webhook` in `Splunk_TA_cisco_meraki` (Splunkbase 5580). Stream-quality issues surface as assurance alerts; specific connection errors come via webhook. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: meraki; **sourcetype**: meraki:assurancealerts, meraki:webhook. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=meraki, sourcetype="meraki:assurancealerts". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where like(deviceTypes, "%camera%") OR like(deviceType, "%camera%") OR productType="camera"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by deviceName, alertType, severity** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where alert_count > 1` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Connection error timeline; affected camera list; error type breakdown.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
